best_evidence_title: Indonesia Sets Path for Climate-Friendly Cooling with ...
best_evidence_query: UNEP capacity building Indonesia scientific assessment after:2022-01-01 before:2026-01-01
best_evidence_capacity_justification: The document provides strong evidence of Indonesia's institutional and technical capacity to develop environmental data systems and policies, specifically through the launch of its first National Cooling Action Plan (I-NCAP). The plan is an inter-ministerial initiative led by the Directorate General of New, Renewable Energy, and Energy Conservation (EBTKE) and includes collaboration from numerous national stakeholders. The I-NCAP focuses on critical areas such as building space cooling, food cold chains, healthcare cold chains, mobile air conditioning, and process cooling. It aims to address increasing cooling demand, enhance energy efficiency in cooling appliances, and promote the use of low-global warming potential (GWP) refrigerants. The plan is aligned with Indonesia's enhanced Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC) and represents a comprehensive policy roadmap to guide Indonesia towards a greener, cooler, and net-zero future.
best_evidence_unep_justification: The document explicitly links UNEP to strengthening or enabling Indonesia's capacity in the development of the National Cooling Action Plan (I-NCAP). UNEP is mentioned as one of the supporters in developing the I-NCAP, along with ESCAP and Sustainable Energy for All. Ruth Zugman do Coutto, Chief of the Mitigation Branch of the Climate Change Division of UNEP, is quoted praising Indonesia's leadership in releasing the plan and encouraging continued collaboration among ministries and stakeholders to pursue its implementation. The document highlights UNEP's role in supporting Indonesia's efforts to address cooling demand and promote climate-friendly cooling solutions.
best_evidence_phrases: ["The I-NCAP is an inter-ministerial policy roadmap led by the Directorate General of New, Renewable Energy, and Energy Conservation (EBTKE) and includes collaboration from numerous national stakeholders.", "Developed with support from the United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific (ESCAP) and the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP) and inputs from Sustainable Energy for All within the framework of the Cool Coalition", "Ruth Zugman do Coutto, Chief of the Mitigation Branch of the Climate Change Division of UNEP said, 'The I-NCAP follows comprehensive vision to delivering on cooling, looking at the multiple cooling sectors to ensure thermal comfort in buildings, strengthening resilience to extreme heat, and bringing essential life‐preserving services like vaccines and safe food to all people while driving climate action.'"]

The project outcomes, such as the installation of 22 weather stations and the issuance of rainfall forecasts and early warning SMS messages, are directly attributed to UNEP's involvement.
best_evidence_phrases: ["The project developed an early warning system for climate change risks, increased capacity for risk responsive planning at the district and local levels, created risk maps and land-use plans to help increase ecosystem and community resilience to climatic shocks, and developed the national information base on climate change and adaptation through awareness and training activities, and the documentation and sharing of lessons learned.", "To enable early warning on climate-change-related risks, the project installed 22 weather stations in 17 of Rwanda’s 30 districts. Each station now provides live data every five minutes, available via the internet and mobile phone.", "The Rwandan Meteorology Agency is now issuing rainfall forecasts three times a day, and sends early warning SMS messages to 1 million people daily.", "Funded by the GEF and implemented by UNEP and UNDP with local partners, the Reducing Vulnerability to Climate Change by Establishing Early Warning and Disaster Preparedness Systems and Support for Integrated Watershed Management in Flood Prone Areas project worked to reduce the vulnerability of people living in the crest area of the Nile-Congo basins, also known as the Gishwati ecosystem."]

best_evidence_title: Rwanda's ambitious plan for clean and efficient cooling
best_evidence_query: UNEP programme Rwanda state of environment report after:2018-01-01 before:2022-01-01
best_evidence_capacity_justification: The document provides evidence of Rwanda's capacity to develop environmental data systems, specifically in the area of cooling and energy efficiency. The Government of Rwanda has released a National Cooling Strategy, which outlines the findings of a recent assessment of the current and future market for cooling products. The strategy includes recommended actions to expand access to cooling while conserving resources. Additionally, Rwanda has developed minimum energy efficiency standards and created financial tools to support businesses investing in clean cooling.
best_evidence_unep_justification: The document explicitly links UNEP to strengthening Rwanda's capacity in the area of cooling and energy efficiency. The Rwanda Cooling Initiative is a joint effort between the Government of Rwanda and UN Environment's United for Efficiency (U4E) team. UNEP's U4E team has worked with the Government of Rwanda to develop the National Cooling Strategy, conduct a cooling market assessment, and create financial tools to support businesses investing in clean cooling. The document quotes Brian Holuj, United for Efficiency's lead for cooling initiatives, highlighting UNEP's role in supporting Rwanda's efforts.
best_evidence_phrases: ["The Rwanda Cooling Initiative, a joint effort between the Government and UN Environment’s United for Efficiency (U4E) team.", "United for Efficiency is working with dozens of countries on sustainable cooling, and there is no better case study than a peer who is taking bold action and showcasing the many benefits.", "The Government of Rwanda has released a landmark plan for sustainably cooling foods and life-saving medicines, and keeping homes and other indoor spaces comfortable in hot weather.", "Rwanda’s National Cooling Strategy outlines the findings of a recent assessment of the current and future market for cooling products.", "Through the Rwanda Cooling Initiative, we have conducted a cooling market assessment, developed a national cooling strategy and minimum energy efficiency standards, and created financial tools to support businesses investing in clean cooling"]
